title: eupatorium album var. glandulosum
intro: Eupatorium album var. glandulosum, also known as the White Snakeroot, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The health benefits of White Snakeroot include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and acting as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of White Snakeroot include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used externally or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
